evals.prompt: A product fixed an iOS crash by adding Expo photo-library and camera permission descriptions. Capture the verified lesson so future projects benefit, but the product name, exact feature copy, and private repository must not be shared.
evals.expected_output: A validated, product-neutral proposal categorized under mobile-app with evidence, reusable permission and native verification rules, and explicit exclusions.
expectations: Targets the exact mobile-app skill rather than a generic learning bucket
Separates reusable permission rules from product-specific wording
Requires native metadata and rebuilt-binary verification
Creates a review proposal and does not directly merge a canonical skill change

evals.prompt: A change fixes a GraphQL nullability mismatch affecting the NestJS server, Next.js web client, and Expo mobile client. Decide where the reusable learning belongs.
evals.expected_output: A narrowly categorized proposal that targets api-app for the schema contract and adds client targets only when each needs a distinct actionable generated-type or error-handling rule.
expectations: Does not choose every involved skill automatically
Explains the responsibility owned by every selected target
Rejects duplicate generic wording across skills
Requires evidence and review before promotion
